name: Orange Trifle
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Packed finely grated orange peel
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium heatproof bowl, whisk the egg yolks with granulated sugar, flour, and cornstarch until thoroughly blended.
2. In a heavy medium saucepan, combine the milk and orange zest and bring to a boil over moderate heat.
3. Gradually whisk the boiling milk into the egg mixture.
4. Strain the custard into the saucepan and bring to a boil over moderate heat, whisking constantly for about 3 minutes.
5. Reduce the heat to moderately low and cook, whisking constantly, for 2 more minutes.
6. Pour the custard into a clean bowl and whisk until slightly cooled, about 1 minute.
7. Let cool to room temperature, whisking occasionally to prevent skin from forming. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (cassert can be made up to 2 days in advance).
8. Using a small sharp knife, peel the oranges, removing all of the bitter white pith. Cut between the membranes to release the segments. You should have 3 cups of orange sections and juice.
9. Cover and refrigerate the oranges up to 2 days ahead.
10. Set aside 12 almond meringues and 1/4 cup walnuts for garnish.
11. Cut the cake into 18 3-inch pieces.
12. Spread one side of each piece with raspberry jam.
13. In a deep 4-quart nonreactive serving bowl, arrange 1/3 of the sponge cake pieces or ladyfingers in a single layer, jam side up. Sprinkle with 2 tbsp sherry.
14. Scatter 1/3 of the orange sections and juice on top.
15. Scatter 1/3 of the remaining meringues between the cake pieces and sprinkle with 1/3 of the remaining walnuts.
16. Spread 1/3 of the custard evenly on top.
17. Repeat layering of sponge cake, sherry, orange sections, meringues, walnuts, and custard two more times.
18. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
19. In a large bowl, whip cream with confectioner's sugar and vanilla until stiff.
20. Spread or pipe whipped cream over the top of the trifle.
21. Refrigerate until serving time (trifle can be prepared up to 4 hours ahead).
22. Just before serving, arrange reserved meringues over the top of the trifle and sprinkle with remaining 1/4 cup walnuts.
